title: cleisostoma brevilabium
intro: Cleisostoma brevilabium, also known as the Dwarf Cleisostoma or Short-lipped Cleisostoma, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Cleisostoma brevilabium have been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, with the leaves and flowers being used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
